DISCOVERY 302, THE PARTIES DID NOT STIPULATE TO THE PT JUDGE. REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION PREPARED. PLAINTIFF WILHELMINA JUI WREN'S (AMENDED) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY FROM DEFENDANT AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, IS CONTINUED TO 7/11/12 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. PRO TEM JUDGE: JAMES WATSON; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF WILHELMINA JUI WREN'S (AMENDED) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY FROM DEFENDANT PACIFIC MARITIME ASSOCIATION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS - GRANTED AS TO MS. WREN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 13 AND 15, AND HER MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DOCUMENT PRODUCTION REQUESTS (SET ONE) 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 AND 38. REGARDING SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 11 AND 12, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED. REGARDING SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 13 AND 15, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED. IF THERE IS NO ONE PERSON DEFENDANT CAN IDENTIFY AS THE "PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE" AFTER A REASONABLE SEARCH, DEFENDANT SHALL STATE SO. REQUEST FOR SANCTION IS DENIED. THE COURT ADDS A DUE DATE OF AUGUST 10, 2012 TO RESPOND. (SEE ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SANDI PETRISCH, CSR # 11684

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2013 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-19-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-05-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2012 Off Calendar

Plaintiff Wilhelmina Jui Wren (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Defendantt And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GLICEL SUMAGAYSAY & ATTY. BETHANY CARACCUZO OF MINAMI & TAMAKI LLP, TEL. NO. 788-9000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ERIC MECKLEY & ATTY. MEGAN BARRY BOROVICKA OF MORGAN LEWIS, TEL. NO. 442-1013 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
SANDI PETRITSCH, CSR. # 11684 TEL. NO. (510) 393-4897, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF WILHELMINA JUI WREN'S (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Defendant Pacific Maritime Association And Request For Sanctions - Granted as to Ms. Wren's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories 13 and 15, and her motion to compel further responses to Document Production Requests (Set One) 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 and 38. Regarding Special Interrogatories 11 and 12, Plaintiff's motion is denied. Regarding Special Interrogatories 13 and 15, Plaintiff's motion is granted. If there is no one person defendant can identify as the "person most knowledgeable" after a reasonable search, defendant shall state so. Request for sanction is denied. The court adds a due date of August 10, 2012 to respond. (See order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sandi Petrisch, CSR # 11684 =(302/HEK)

(Amended) Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Disco From Deft And Req For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bethany Caracuzzo, Esq., and Glicel Sumagaysay, Esq., from Minami Tamaki LLP, 415-788-9000, for plaintiff and moving party
Megan Barry Borovicka, Esq., from Morgan Lewis & Bockius, LLP, 415-442-1318, for defendant and opposing party

The parties did not stipulate to the PT Judge. Report and Recommendation prepared.
PLAINTIFF WILHELMINA JUI WREN'S (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Defendant And Request for Sanctions, is continued to 7/11/12 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302.
Pro Tem Judge: James Watson; Not reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Deft'S Request For Sanctions
